metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and correct the composition-point claim: confirm whether the preamble injection at `_resolve_system` (which resolves pass-level prompts like plan_review_finder.md) actually reaches the 41 per-criterion plan_review_*.md files, or whether criterion files must be edited directly / a second injection point added (e.g. in `_criterion_block` or the rubric assembly at passes.py:267–271).
findings.finding: The plan asserts that injecting the shared preamble at `_resolve_system` covers 'the 41 src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_*.md files', but `_resolve_system` is only called with PASS-level prompt IDs (PASS_FINDER='plan-review-finder', PASS_CONTAINER, PASS_ISF, PASS_COMPLETION, PASS_COACH, PASS_NOVELTY) — resolving ~6 pass-level prompts (plan_review_finder.md, plan_review_container.md, etc.), NOT the 41 individual criterion files. The per-criterion files (plan_review_E1.md, plan_review_T1.md, etc.) are loaded by the registry and assembled into the `instructions` field of each RunRequest, bypassing _resolve_system entirely. A preamble injected at _resolve_system therefore reaches only the pass-level system prompts, not the criterion text the reviewer actually reads during each rubric chunk.
findings.location: What section ('Composition point ALREADY EXISTS: _resolve_system(prompt_id, plan, cfg) at passes.py:237 assembles the system prompt. Add the shared preamble THERE') + AC1 ('Shared preamble injected via _resolve_system (passes.py:237); injection directive + forward-looking rule live there once')
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is to harden the 41 criterion files, edit them directly (the affirmative-framing sweep in AC2 already implies direct edits). For the G-12/FP-3(c) directives that belong in a shared preamble, add them to the finder/container/ISF pass prompts (the actual _resolve_system targets), and update AC1's proof to assert those ~6 pass prompts contain the preamble — not all 41 reviewer files.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC1's scope: either (a) restate the proof assertion as 'every pass-level system prompt (finder, verifier, coach, container, ISF-finder, novelty, completion) resolves with the preamble prepended' — which matches what `_resolve_system` actually touches — or (b) specify a second injection path for the 41 criterion files if that coverage is intended.
findings.finding: AC1's proof assertion — '`pytest -k resolve_system_preamble` asserts every plan_review reviewer resolves with the preamble prepended' — overstates what `_resolve_system` touches. The function resolves 7 pass-level prompts (plan-review-finder, plan-review-verifier, plan-review-coach, plan-review-container, plan-review-isf-finder, plan-review-novelty, plan-review-completion); it does NOT resolve the 41 individual criterion files (plan_review_A1.md, plan_review_E1.md, etc.), which are injected as `instructions` (not system prompts) inside `pass1_chunk`. A test asserting 'every plan_review reviewer' will either miss the criterion files entirely or require a different injection mechanism to cover them.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 (shared preamble injection proof) and What section ('_resolve_system … assembles the system prompt')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify injection scope: _resolve_system reaches only 6 pass-level prompts, not the 41 criterion .md files; either (a) update AC1 to claim coverage of the 6 pass prompts only, or (b) define the correct injection point (e.g. the per-criterion .md files themselves, or the _criterion_block() assembly) that actually reaches all 41.
findings.finding: _resolve_system is only called for the 6 pass-level prompts (finder, container, ISF, completion, novelty, coach); the 41 plan_review_*.md criterion files are rendered into the `instructions` field via _criterion_block() and never pass through _resolve_system. AC1's test assertion ('every plan_review reviewer resolves with the preamble prepended') therefore cannot be satisfied by injecting at _resolve_system — the injection would only reach the 6 pass-level system prompts, leaving all 41 criterion files without the preamble.
findings.location: AC1 / What section ('Composition point ALREADY EXISTS: _resolve_system') / passes.py:269,356,429,578,781
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is to protect the 41 criterion .md files, the preamble text must be prepended directly in each .md file (or in the frontmatter/template mechanism that renders them), not in _resolve_system. If the intent is only to protect the 6 pass-level prompts, update the plan scope and AC1 to say '6 pass-level prompts' rather than '41 src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_*.md files'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the preamble injected at `_resolve_system` reaches the 6 pass-level system prompts (finder, verifier, coach, etc.) — not the 41 criterion overlay files — and revise AC1's proof claim ('every plan_review reviewer') to match the actual injection scope, or separately specify how G-12/FP-3(c) directives reach the criterion rubric blocks.
findings.finding: The preamble injected at `_resolve_system` reaches only the 6 pass-level system prompts (finder, container, ISF, completion, novelty, coach/verifier), not the 41 `plan_review_*.md` criterion files. The criterion files are rendered as rubric blocks inside the `instructions` field of each RunRequest — they never pass through `_resolve_system`. The G-12 injection directive and FP-3(c) forward-looking rule therefore land in the finder's system-role context but are absent from the criterion overlays themselves. If the goal is to harden the criterion files (e.g. T8, F1…) against DO-NOT accumulation and instruction injection, injecting the preamble at `_resolve_system` does not achieve that for the criterion layer.
findings.location: What section — 'Composition point ALREADY EXISTS: _resolve_system ... Add the shared preamble THERE'; AC1 — 'Shared preamble injected via _resolve_system (passes.py:237) ... Proof: pytest -k resolve_system_preamble asserts every plan_review reviewer resolves with the preamble prepended'
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) scope AC1's claim correctly to 'the 6 pass-level system prompts' and accept that the G-12/FP-3(c) preamble lives in the finder's system role (where it guards against plan-injected instructions), treating the 41-file sweep (AC2) as the separate hygiene mechanism it already is; or (b) if the intent is for G-12/FP-3(c) to also appear in criterion files, add them to a shared criterion preamble block directly in those files (or document that the finder system-prompt's preamble already covers the model's interpretation context for all criterion content it receives).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (or add) that the eval-spec harness has per-criterion recall cases dense enough to detect behavioral regressions from rewording the 41 plan_review_*.md files, or explicitly scope AC3 to 'pass-level prompt regression only' and document what coverage gap remains for criterion-file rewrites.
findings.finding: AC3 asserts the 'EVAL-SPEC harness (eval_specs/plan-review-*.eval.yaml)' proves that reworded prompts keep prior recall, but the actual eval specs cover only pass-level prompts (plan-review-finder, plan-review-verifier, plan-review-container, plan-review-isf-finder, plan-review-novelty) — not the 41 individual plan_review_*.md criterion files whose content the affirmative-framing sweep (AC2) modifies. This is an unverified capability assertion: the plan treats the existing harness as sufficient regression coverage for 41 criterion-file rewrites without confirming that the per-criterion recall cases in plan-review-finder.eval.yaml are dense enough to catch behavioral regressions from individual criterion-prompt rewording.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 ('Eval/sidecar regression on the EVAL-SPEC harness')
findings.suggested_fix: In AC3, add a sentence clarifying the harness scope: 'Note: the eval specs exercise pass-level prompts; criterion-file rewording regressions are caught indirectly via the finder's seeded per-criterion recall cases. Confirm at least one seeded recall case per criterion touched by the sweep, or add cases before sweeping.' This resolves the unverified capability assertion without requiring new infrastructure.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how AC3's recall-regression proof applies to the 41 criterion .md files: either (a) name a substitute proof mechanism (e.g., a spot-eval or manual review sampling criterion), or (b) acknowledge that the existing eval harness covers only pass-level prompts and the regression gate applies only to those.
findings.finding: AC3 references 'eval_specs/plan-review-*.eval.yaml' as the proof mechanism that reworded prompts keep prior recall, but the existing eval harness covers only 5 pass-level prompts (finder, verifier, isf-finder, container, novelty); there are no per-criterion eval specs for the 41 plan_review_*.md files. If the sweep rewrites criterion .md bodies, the recall regression test AC3 claims does not exist for those files.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a described work step in the What section for capturing and recording the eval-spec pass-rate on the 41 unmodified prompts *before* any edits begin, so AC3's 'record both' requirement has a corresponding deliverable.
findings.finding: AC3 requires recording a pre-sweep eval baseline ('record both') and comparing pass-rates, but the plan body (What section) contains no described work step for establishing or storing that baseline before the sweep begins. This leaves the AC verifying a measurement that has no corresponding described deliverable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 ('Proof: eval-spec pass-rate >= pre-sweep baseline (record both)') vs. What section (no baseline-capture step described)
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add a step such as: 'Before any prompt edits: run `pytest -k eval_spec_plan_review` and record the baseline pass-rate to `eval_baselines/ws7_pre_sweep.json`; AC3 comparison uses this artifact.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete step (and named artifact location) for capturing the pre-sweep eval baseline BEFORE any prompt edits, so AC 3's comparison is deterministic and in-session completable.
findings.finding: AC 3 references 'pre-sweep baseline' but no step or mechanism is specified for capturing that baseline before changes begin, making the criterion incompletable in-session if the baseline does not already exist.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC 3: 'eval-spec pass-rate >= pre-sweep baseline (record both)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add: 'Step 0 — Record baseline: run `pytest --eval-harness eval_specs/plan-review-*.eval.yaml --record-baseline baseline.json` (or equivalent) BEFORE any file edits; store result at eval_specs/baseline.json (committed or CI-cached). AC 3 comparison reads from that file.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit step (and corresponding AC or scripted check) to remove the FP-3(c) text from plan_review_F1.md after hoisting it to the shared preamble, ensuring the rule appears exactly once in F1's assembled system prompt.
findings.finding: The plan says FP-3(c) 'lives in plan_review_F1.md today — hoist to the shared preamble,' but does not specify whether the original copy in F1.md will be REMOVED after hoisting. If it is not removed, the rule will appear twice in every F1 reviewer's assembled system prompt (once from the preamble, once from the file), which could cause redundancy or, worse, contradictory future edits in two places.
findings.location: What section — 'FP-3(c) forward-looking rule … Verified it lives in plan_review_F1.md today (not E1) -- hoist to the shared preamble'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the back-out description to explicitly include reverting the CONTRIBUTING.md change, or specify that all WS7 changes (preamble hook, prompt edits, CONTRIBUTING.md) land in a single revertible commit/branch.
findings.finding: The back-out description names exactly two artifacts to revert ('passes.py preamble hook + prompt edits — one commit') but omits the CONTRIBUTING.md addition required by AC4. If a regression triggers revert, CONTRIBUTING.md retains the checklist entry while the feature it documents no longer exists, leaving the repo in an inconsistent state.
findings.location: What section — back-out description ('revert the passes.py preamble hook + prompt edits (one commit)') vs. AC4 ('Review-time checklist location documented (CONTRIBUTING.md)')
findings.suggested_fix: Update the back-out sentence to: 'Back-out: revert the passes.py preamble hook, prompt edits, and CONTRIBUTING.md addition (one commit or one branch revert).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the eval-spec path in AC3 from `eval_specs/plan-review-*.eval.yaml` to `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-*.eval.yaml` (or equivalently the package-relative path `rebar/llm/eval_specs/`).
findings.finding: AC3 references `eval_specs/plan-review-*.eval.yaml` as the path for the EVAL-SPEC harness, but the actual location is `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-*.eval.yaml`. The path missing the `src/rebar/llm/` prefix means any CI command, script, or grep anchored to the AC-quoted path would resolve to a non-existent directory.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the exact recall metric for AC 3 (e.g., name the YAML key, formula, or threshold), or confirm that 'recall' is synonymous with 'pass-rate' as used earlier in the same AC.
findings.finding: The 'recall' metric cited in AC 3 ('reworded prompts keep prior recall') is not defined anywhere in the plan; without knowing how recall is computed, an agent cannot measure or confirm the criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC 3: 'reworded prompts keep prior recall'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the eval harness (AC 3) runs against a live model or a mock, and if the latter, add at least one AC that exercises `_resolve_system` + a real LLM call end-to-end (even a single canary call) to satisfy the cutover criterion.
findings.finding: The pytest proving command for AC 1 (`pytest -k resolve_system_preamble`) is an offline assembly test; the plan names no in-production or live-model check that exercises the injected preamble as it will actually run against a real LLM, leaving the cutover path unverified end-to-end.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC 1 + AC 3 combined; 'What' section — preamble injection via _resolve_system

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the shared-preamble injection decision in CLAUDE.md or an ADR (scope, rationale, which reviewer families are covered, how to extend), not just in CONTRIBUTING.md's review-time checklist.
findings.finding: The shared preamble injection at _resolve_system is a novel pipeline-stage decision affecting all 41+ plan_review_*.md reviewers, but the plan names no ADR, CLAUDE.md section, or design-doc update to capture it — only CONTRIBUTING.md is mentioned (for the review-time checklist habit, not the architectural decision).
findings.location: ## What / 'Composition point ALREADY EXISTS' paragraph; AC bullet 4
findings.suggested_fix: Add an ADR or a `## Architecture` section in CLAUDE.md that documents: (1) _resolve_system is the single injection point for shared reviewer preamble, (2) the G-12 and FP-3(c) directives and their rationale, (3) how to add or update the preamble text (file location, format), and (4) which reviewer families are in/out of scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `_resolve_system` guards preamble injection to `plan_review_*` prompt IDs only (or confirm it is plan-review-only), and add a negative assertion to the AC test that code-review reviewers do NOT receive the plan-review preamble.
findings.finding: The plan injects the shared preamble at `_resolve_system` (passes.py:237), a function that likely serves ALL reviewer types (plan-review AND the ~18 code-review reviewers), yet the plan declares code-review reviewers 'out of scope.' If `_resolve_system` is truly shared, the preamble (including the G-12 injection directive and the FP-3(c) forward-looking rule) will silently be prepended to code-review reviewer prompts as well, expanding scope beyond what the plan states.
findings.location: What section — 'Composition point ALREADY EXISTS: _resolve_system(prompt_id, plan, cfg) at passes.py:237' and 'Scope = the 41 src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_*.md files'
findings.suggested_fix: In passes.py, gate the preamble prepend on `prompt_id.startswith('plan_review_')` (or equivalent). Add a complementary pytest assertion: `assert preamble not in resolve_system('code_review_X', ...)`.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
